content.full_text:
February 28,
1995
David R.E.
Aladjem
Downey,
Brand,
Seymour
& Rohwer
555 Capitol Mall,
10th Floor
Sacramento,
CA
95814—4686
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A-95—57
Dear Mr. Aladjem:
This is in response to your request for advice under the
conflict—of-interest disclosure provisions of the Political Reform
Act.’! This letter will also to serve to confirm telephone advice.
QUESTION
Are persons who are enumerated as “other public officials who
manage public investments” in Government Code Section 87200
required to file Form 721 or Form 730?
CONCLUSION
Persons who are specifically enumerated in Section 87200,
including “other public officials who manage public investments”
disclose their personal economic interests on Form 721.
DISCUSSION
Commission Regulation 18720(b)
defines “other public
officials who manage public investments,” as that term is used in
Section 87200 to include:
(1)
Members of boards and commissions,
including
pension or retirement boards or commissions,
or of
committees thereof,
who exercise responsibility for the
management of public investments;
1/
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations,
Sections 18000-18954.
All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.


File No.
A—95-57
Page
2
(2)
High-level officers and employees of public
agencies who exercise primary responsibility for the
management of public investments,
such as chief or
principal investment officers or chief financial
managers.
This category shall not include officers and
employees who work under the supervision of the chief or
principal investment officers or the chief financial
managers;
and
(3)
Individuals who, pursuant to a contract with a
state or local government agency, perform the same or
substantially all the same functions that would
otherwise be performed by the public officials described
in subdivision
(b) (2)
above.
If the officers or employees mentioned in your letter meet
the above definition, they are “other public officials who manage
public investments.”
Public officials who fall into this
definition are sub5ect to the conflict-of-interest disclosure
requirements of Article 2,
Chapter
7 of the Act and must file a
Form 721.
(See Comment: Regulation 18720.)
However,
I cannot confirm the information contained in the
rest of your letter.
My notes indicate that I actually advised
you that the Act does not require the identification of any
specific form,
by number,
in the conflict of interest code and
that if your codes make such a reference to Form 730,
such
reference was not necessary and could be deleted.
I further
informed you that the staff has engaged in initial discussions to
consider consolidating the two forms to alleviate any confusion.
This proposal has not as yet been considered by the Commission.
I hope to this clarifies and confirms our conversation.
Please feel free to contact the Commission’s Legal Division if you
have further questions.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
Jeanette E. Turvill
Political Reform Consultant
Legal Division
JET/jt

QUESTION: Are persons who are enumerated as “other public officials who
manage public investments” in Government Code Section 87200
required to file Form 721 or Form 730?

CONCLUSION: Persons who are specifically enumerated in Section 87200,
including “other public officials who manage public investments”
disclose their personal economic interests on Form 721.
